title: Peace Corps Volunteer
description: Two-year U.S. government volunteer assignment in agriculture, education, health, or community development projects, currently posted in roughly 60 countries worldwide -- including Ghana (the first country to host Peace Corps Volunteers, since 1961), Vietnam, Fiji, Mongolia, and the Kyrgyz Republic among many others. Country placement is assigned by Peace Corps based on program needs and applicant background, not chosen upfront by the applicant. Peace Corps books flights, provides housing and a monthly living allowance, full medical/dental coverage, and a readjustment allowance (currently $10,000 pre-tax for a full 27-month term) paid at the end of service.
visaNote: Peace Corps arranges necessary legal status/visa with the Ghanaian government as part of placement; volunteers do not apply independently.

title: Australian Volunteers Program — South Africa
description: Australian Government (DFAT)-funded skilled-volunteer assignments matching Australian professionals with partner organisations across Africa, Asia, and the Pacific (including South Africa) to support locally led development. Assignments typically run 1–24 months (commonly around 12); the program covers flights, accommodation/living allowance, and insurance, with no cost to the volunteer. Country placement depends on where a matching assignment is currently open.
visaNote: Work/volunteer visa arranged with support from the Australian Volunteers Program and host-country partner.

title: PALM Scheme — Seasonal & Long-Term Work in Australia
description: Australian Government labour mobility scheme (Subclass 403 visa) that places workers from Fiji, Kiribati, Nauru, Papua New Guinea, Samoa, Solomon Islands, Timor-Leste, Tonga, Tuvalu, and Vanuatu with approved employers in Australian agriculture, hospitality, and other sectors. Short-term placements run up to 9 months and long-term placements 1–4 years; employers arrange visas and pay award wages, and can recover part of travel costs through wage deductions (minus a compulsory $300 employer contribution). (This app currently supports a limited set of nationalities in its filters; the real program's eligibility is broader than what's listed here.)
org: Australian Government — Pacific Australia Labour Mobility (PALM) scheme
visaNote: Subclass 403 Temporary Work (International Relations) visa, PALM scheme stream; sponsored by an approved employer.

title: VSO International Volunteer — Kenya
description: Voluntary Service Overseas places experienced professionals in 1–2 year placements supporting health, education, and livelihoods programs in Kenya, where VSO has worked since 1959. VSO covers flights, accommodation, a living allowance in local currency, insurance, and at least 3 weeks annual leave; applicants need a degree or equivalent and at least three years' relevant professional experience.
visaNote: VSO arranges the necessary work/residence permit in Kenya as part of placement.

title: Camphill Village South Africa — Young Co-Worker Program
description: Year-long live-in volunteer program at Camphill Village, a life-sharing community for adults with intellectual disabilities in South Africa, open to both international and local young people aged 18-25. Volunteers receive free private room in shared housing, meals, and a modest stipend for incidental expenses in exchange for daily work and life alongside residents.
visaNote: South African volunteer visa arranged with Camphill Village's support.

title: weltwärts Süd-Nord — Volunteer Service in Germany
description: German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ) program bringing young people aged 18-29 from Global South partner countries to Germany for 6-18 months of voluntary service in fields like education, health, environment, culture, or human rights. Applicants apply through a partner organisation in their home country working with a German host organisation; the program covers a monthly stipend, accommodation, travel, and insurance.
org: weltwärts (German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development)
visaNote: National visa/residence permit for voluntary service in Germany, arranged with the sending and receiving organisations' support.

title: AmeriCorps NCCC — Team-Based National Service
description: AmeriCorps' National Civilian Community Corps places 18-26 year-olds in team-based, residential national service for about 10 months, rotating through disaster response, conservation, and community-strengthening projects across the U.S. Members live together in provided housing, receive meals/a living allowance (about $4,000 total), full-time team travel to project sites, and a Segal Education Award (about $4,725) upon completion.

title: UN Volunteer Assignments — Uzbekistan
description: United Nations Volunteers places national and international UN Volunteers with UN agencies operating in Uzbekistan, including UNDP-backed social innovation and youth development projects. UNV assignments provide a Volunteer Living Allowance (set per duty station to cover basic living costs, not a salary), insurance, and travel to the duty station for international assignments; a higher diploma or degree plus working knowledge of English, French, or Spanish is required, with age brackets varying by volunteer category (18-26 for Youth/University Volunteers, 27-80 for Specialists).
visaNote: UNV and the host UN agency arrange the necessary visa or UN volunteer identification for the assignment.
